claim_bullets.bullet_text: Iterators are a JavaScript feature that allow us to separate the concerns of how to iterate over a collection from what we want to do with the elements of a collection. Iterable ordered collections can be iterated over or gathered into another collection. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.223-223)

Separating concerns with iterators speaks to JavaScript's fundamental nature: It's a language that wants to compose functionality out of small, singe-responsibility pieces, whether those pieces are functions or objects built out of functions. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.223-223)

source_record_text: JavaScript Allongé explores fundamental and advanced JavaScript concepts, including logical operators, control flow mechanisms, and linear recursion.
